bead.title: ddx home: route residual state-root consumers through ddxroot
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The landed zero-footprint refactor introduced `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)` and a pre-commit audit, but the audit only catches `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...)`. Production code still has direct `.ddx` state-root consumers that will keep reading or committing the project worktree instead of the XDG state repository in convention mode.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1791-1837` stats the tracker via `ddxroot.JoinProject(projectRoot, "beads.jsonl")`, but then checks/stages/commits literal `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in `projectRoot`. In convention mode that never commits the XDG tracker repo.
`cli/internal/attemptmetrics/attemptmetrics.go:19-101` defines `.ddx/metrics/attempts.jsonl` as a relative constant and joins it directly under `projectRoot`, bypassing `ddxroot`.
`cli/cmd/work_plan.go:82-87` and `cli/cmd/work_clear_cooldowns.go:46-47` open `projectRoot + "/.ddx"` directly, so convention-mode projects show empty or wrong queues.
The new audit command in `lefthook.yml` only searches for `filepath.Join(..., ".ddx")`, so these regressions are invisible to pre-commit.

PROPOSED FIX
Route the concrete residual state-root consumers above through `ddxroot.Path` / `ddxroot.JoinProject`. When committing tracker state in convention mode, run git in the actual DDx state root repository and stage `beads.jsonl` relative to that repo; preserve existing in-tree behavior and execute-bead merge-policy commits. Update the audit so it fails on production direct state-root patterns such as `projectRoot + "/.ddx"`, `WorkingDir + "/.ddx"`, and constants joined under projectRoot for DDx-managed state, while still allowing relative artifact path strings used inside worker prompts and git pathspec filters.

NON-SCOPE
Do not migrate this repository out of in-tree mode. Do not rewrite prompt text that intentionally tells agents where evidence appears in an execution worktree. Do not change the bead storage format or attachment externalization policy.
bead.acceptance:
1. Add `TestCommitTrackerConventionModeCommitsXDGTracker` proving `CommitTracker(projectRoot)` commits `beads.jsonl` in the XDG ddxroot repository when `projectRoot` has no `.ddx/`, and does not try to stage `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in the project worktree.
2. Add `TestAttemptMetricsUseDDxRootPath` proving `attemptmetrics.AppendRow`, `LoadRows`, and `LoadAttemptIDs` read/write `${ddxroot.Path(projectRoot)}/metrics/attempts.jsonl` in convention mode.
3. Add `TestWorkPlanUsesDDxRootPath` and `TestWorkClearCooldownsUsesDDxRootPath` proving those commands read the convention-mode bead store via `ddxroot.Path` rather than `projectRoot/.ddx`.
4. Harden the `ddxroot-path-audit` pre-commit/CI rule so a production Go file containing `projectRoot + "/.ddx"`, `WorkingDir + "/.ddx"`, or an equivalent DDx-managed state root bypass fails outside `cli/internal/ddxroot/` and documented test fixtures.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent ./internal/attemptmetrics ./cmd -run 'TestCommitTrackerConventionModeCommitsXDGTracker|TestAttemptMetricsUseDDxRootPath|TestWorkPlanUsesDDxRootPath|TestWorkClearCooldownsUsesDDxRootPath' -count=1` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
